faq_text:
Q: Do I need to bake this Easy No-Bake Biscoff Cheesecake?
A: No—this is a true no-bake cheesecake, so there’s no oven step. The texture comes from whipped cream folded into a cream cheese base and then chilled in a springform pan. Make sure the crust is pressed firmly so it holds together when sliced.

Q: What makes the cheesecake “no-bake” and still firm enough to slice?
A: The filling is stabilized by the combination of cream cheese and heavy whipping cream whipped to stiff peaks. Folding the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture creates a thick, airy filling that sets as it chills. Using cold cream and beating to stiff peaks is key.

Q: Why do the ingredients specify softened cream cheese and cold heavy cream?
A: Softened cream cheese beats smooth without lumps, which keeps the filling creamy. Cold heavy whipping cream whips more easily and holds stiff peaks better. Using them at these temperatures helps the filling mix evenly and set properly.

Q: How do I warm the Biscoff cookie butter for the filling without making it too hot?
A: Warm it just enough to loosen it so it folds in easily. It should be slightly warmed and pourable, not hot. This helps you create the light swirl in the filling without thinning the mixture too much.

Q: Can I use Biscoff spread and Biscoff cookie butter interchangeably in this recipe?
A: Yes—Biscoff spread is the same type of product often labeled as cookie butter. The recipe uses 1 cup slightly warmed cookie butter in the filling and an extra 1/4 cup for drizzling. Just measure the amounts as listed.

Q: How do I get clean slices when serving no-bake Biscoff cheesecake?
A: Release the springform side carefully and slice with a sharp knife. Wipe the knife between cuts to keep the edges neat, especially with the cookie butter swirl and drizzles. Topping with crushed Biscoff cookies is best done right before slicing so it stays crisp.

Q: What’s the best way to fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture?
A: Fold gently with a spatula until no streaks remain, rather than mixing aggressively. This keeps the whipped cream from deflating, which helps the cheesecake stay light and set properly. Scrape the sides of the bowl so everything is evenly combined.

Q: About how many cookies do I need to make 1 1/2 cups of Biscoff crumbs for the crust?
A: The recipe notes that 1 1/2 cups of Biscoff cookie crumbs is about 25 cookies. Crushing them into fine crumbs helps the melted butter bind the crust well. Press the mixture firmly into the pan so it holds together.
recipe_text:
Title: Easy No-Bake Biscoff Cheesecake
Description: Make this decadent No-Bake Biscoff Cheesecake for a creamy, rich dessert that requires no oven time. It features a simple Biscoff cookie crust and a luscious cookie butter filling, perfect for parties or any sweet craving.
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 0 min
Total: 5 hr
Servings/Yield: 10 servings
Calories: 450 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 1/2 cups Biscoff cookie crumbs (about 25 cookies)
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 6 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
- 2 (8 ounce) packages cream cheese, softened
- 1 cup powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
- 1 cup heavy whipping cream, cold
- 1 cup Biscoff cookie butter, slightly warmed
- 1/4 cup Biscoff cookie butter, for drizzling
- 1/4 cup crushed Biscoff cookies, for topping

Instructions:
1. Make the cream cheese base: In a large bowl, use an electric mixer to beat the softened cream cheese until it is smooth and creamy. Add the powdered sugar and vanilla bean paste, mixing until just combined. Scrape down the sides of the bowl.
2. Whip the cream: In a separate, clean bowl, beat the cold heavy whipping cream until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture until no streaks remain.
3. Incorporate the cookie butter: Take about half of the slightly warmed Biscoff cookie butter and gently fold it into the cream cheese mixture until it creates a light swirl. Do not overmix; you want ribbons of flavor.
4. Assemble the cheesecake: Pour the filling over the chilled crust in the springform pan. Smooth the top with a spatula.
5. Serve: Before serving your Easy Biscoff Cheesecake, carefully release the springform side. Top with crushed Biscoff cookies. Slice and serve this indulgent sweet treat.

topic: Paleo, Keto, & Sugar Free No Bake Cookies
faq_text:
Q: Are these Paleo, Keto, & Sugar Free No Bake Cookies truly no-bake?
A: Yes—there’s no oven baking required. You gently heat the almond butter (or peanut butter), coconut oil, monkfruit syrup, cacao, and vanilla on the stovetop just until melted and combined, then chill the cookies to set.

Q: What makes these no bake cookies keto and sugar free?
A: They’re sweetened with Lakanto maple-flavored monkfruit syrup instead of sugar. The base is almond butter, coconut oil, cacao, and shredded coconut, which fits a low-carb, keto-friendly ingredient list.

Q: Can I use peanut butter instead of almond butter?
A: Yes, peanut butter works in the same amount as almond butter. However, the recipe notes that using peanut butter makes the cookies not paleo.

Q: Can I swap shredded coconut for oats in these no bake cookies?
A: Yes, you can use gluten-free oats instead of shredded coconut. The recipe notes that using oats makes them not paleo and not low carb.

Q: How long do Paleo, Keto, & Sugar Free No Bake Cookies need to chill before they’re firm?
A: They can be refrigerated for about 1 hour to become solid. For faster setting, place them in the freezer for about 20 minutes.

Q: Why does the mixture need to cool and thicken before scooping?
A: Cooling for about 20–30 minutes helps the mixture thicken so it holds its shape when scooped. Stirring during this cooling time helps it cool faster and stay evenly mixed.

Q: How do I keep the mixture from burning on the stove?
A: Heat it over medium-low and stir continually as the ingredients melt. Continuous stirring prevents the mixture from sticking and burning on the bottom or sides of the pan.

Q: What’s the best way to form and store these cookies?
A: Scoop portions onto a parchment-lined cookie sheet, then chill until firm. After they set, store them in the refrigerator to keep them solid and easy to handle.
recipe_text:
Title: Paleo, Keto, & Sugar Free No Bake Cookies
Description: These paleo, keto, and sugar free no-bake cookies are a delicious, healthy, fill-your-tummy-treat; perfect for a little indulgence without wrecking your diet.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 5 min
Total: 10 min
Servings/Yield: 18 servings
Calories: 269 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 cup almond butter ((or peanut butter, but not paleo))
- ½ cup Lakanto maple syrup flavored monkfruit
- ½ cup coconut oil
- 5 tablespoons raw cacao
- 1 tablespoon vanilla bean paste
- 2 1/2 cups shredded coconut ((or gluten free oats, but not paleo or low carb))

Instructions:
1. Heat the peanut butter, syrup, cacao, vanilla, and coconut oil together on the stove over med-low heat.
2. Stir it continually it so it melts quickly and blends well, but doesn't burn to the bottom or sides.
3. Remove the mixture from the heat, and add in the shredded coconut.
4. Gently stir in the shredded coconut, and mix well to ensure there are no clumps.
5. Let the cookie mixture cool until it thickens, approximately 20-30 minutes. Stirring helps it cool faster.
6. Using a large spoon, put scoops of the cookie mixture on a parchment-lined cookie sheet.
7. Put in the fridge until solid. Cookies may be refrigerated for an hour, or put in the freezer for 20 minutes.

faq_text:
Q: Do No-Bake Strawberry Cheesecake Bars need to be baked at all?
A: No, these No-Bake Strawberry Cheesecake Bars don’t require any baking. The graham cracker crust is simply mixed and chilled, and the filling is made by combining cream cheese with whipped heavy cream. Chilling helps the layers hold their shape for slicing.

Q: How do I keep the graham cracker crust from crumbling?
A: Mix the graham cracker crumbs thoroughly with the melted butter until the texture resembles wet sand. Press the mixture firmly into the pan so it compacts into an even layer. Refrigerating the crust while you prepare the filling also helps it set up.

Q: Why does the recipe specify softened cream cheese?
A: Softened cream cheese beats more smoothly, which helps prevent lumps in the filling. Cold cream cheese can stay chunky even with mixing. Letting it soften makes it easier to combine evenly with the powdered sugar and vanilla bean paste.

Q: What does whipping the heavy cream to stiff peaks do for the filling?
A: Whipped heavy cream adds structure and a lighter texture to the cheesecake layer. Reaching stiff peaks ensures the cream has enough stability to hold its shape when folded into the cream cheese mixture. Folding gently helps keep the filling smooth and airy.

Q: Can I use vanilla extract instead of vanilla bean paste?
A: Yes, you can swap vanilla extract for vanilla bean paste in these No-Bake Strawberry Cheesecake Bars. The vanilla provides flavor in the filling either way. Use the same 1 teaspoon measurement for a straightforward substitution.

Q: How should I incorporate the strawberries for No-Bake Strawberry Cheesecake Bars?
A: The recipe calls for fresh strawberries that are hulled and chopped. You can spoon the chopped strawberries over the cheesecake layer as a topping or distribute them as a layer, depending on your pan and serving style. Keeping the pieces chopped helps them sit evenly across the bars.

Q: Why does the filling get folded instead of fully mixed?
A: Folding the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture helps maintain the air you whipped into the cream. If you mix aggressively, the whipped cream can deflate and the filling can become denser. Gentle folding creates a smooth, light cheesecake layer.

Q: How many servings does this No-Bake Strawberry Cheesecake Bars recipe make?
A: This recipe makes 9 servings. That typically corresponds to cutting the dessert into a 3-by-3 grid of equal bars. For cleaner slices, smooth the top with a spatula before portioning.
recipe_text:
Title: No-Bake Strawberry Cheesecake Bars
Description: These No-Bake Strawberry Cheesecake Bars are the perfect creamy and refreshing treat! With a buttery graham cracker crust, a luscious cheesecake filling, and a sweet strawberry topping, they’re ideal for any occasion.
Prep: 25 min
Cook: 10 min
Total: 25 min
Servings/Yield: 9 servings
Calories: 420 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 ½ cups graham cracker crumbs (about 12 graham crackers)
- ⅓ cup granulated sugar
- ½ cup unsalted butter, melted
- 2 cups cream cheese, softened
- 1 cup powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
- 2 cups heavy cream
- 2 cups fresh strawberries, hulled and chopped
- ¼ cup granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice

Instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the graham cracker crumbs and granulated sugar.
2. Stir in the melted butter until everything is well combined and the mixture resembles wet sand.
3. Refrigerate the crust while you prepare the filling.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat the softened cream cheese with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy.
5. Gradually add powdered sugar and vanilla bean paste, continuing to beat until well combined.
6. In a separate bowl, whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form. Be careful not to over-whip.
7. Gently fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture until fully incorporated and smooth.
8. Spread the cheesecake filling evenly over the chilled crust. Use a spatula to smooth the top.
9. In a small saucepan, combine the chopped strawberries, granulated sugar, and lemon juice.
10. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the strawberries release their juices and the mixture becomes syrupy, about 5-7 minutes.
11. Remove from heat and let the strawberry topping cool to room temperature. If you’d prefer a smoother topping, you can mash the strawberries lightly with a fork or blend them for a smoother consistency.
12. Once the strawberry topping has cooled, spoon it evenly over the cheesecake layer.
13. Refrigerate the bars for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the layers to set.
14. When ready to serve, cut into squares or bars. Enjoy the creamy, refreshing flavor of these no-bake cheesecake bars!

faq_text:
Q: Why are these called Classic No-Bake Cookies?
A: Classic No-Bake Cookies are made by boiling a chocolate-sugar mixture on the stove and mixing it with peanut butter and oats, so there’s no oven baking involved. They set up as they cool on wax paper or parchment paper.

Q: Do I need quick-cooking oats for Classic No-Bake Cookies?
A: Yes, this recipe is written for quick-cooking oats, which absorb the hot mixture quickly and help the cookies set. Using a different type of oat can change the texture and how well they firm up.

Q: How long should I boil the mixture for no-bake cookies?
A: Once the mixture reaches a full rolling boil, boil it for exactly 1 minute while stirring continuously. That timing helps the cookies set properly as they cool.

Q: How do I know when the mixture is at a full rolling boil?
A: A full rolling boil means the mixture is bubbling actively across the surface and continues to boil even as you stir. Start timing the 1 minute only after it reaches that stage.

Q: Can I use creamy or chunky peanut butter in Classic No-Bake Cookies?
A: Yes, the recipe works with either creamy or chunky peanut butter. Creamy makes a smoother cookie, while chunky adds small peanut pieces for more texture.

Q: Can I substitute vanilla extract for vanilla bean paste?
A: Yes, you can use vanilla extract in place of vanilla bean paste in the same 1 teaspoon amount. The flavor will still be vanilla-forward, but you won’t get the speckled look that paste can add.

Q: How long do Classic No-Bake Cookies take to set?
A: They typically set at room temperature in 20–30 minutes. If you want them firmer sooner, refrigerate them to speed up setting.

Q: What’s the best surface to drop no-bake cookies onto?
A: Wax paper or parchment paper works best because the cookies release easily once set. Line a baking sheet or a clean countertop before scooping the cookies.

Q: How many cookies does this Classic No-Bake Cookies recipe make?
A: This recipe yields about 24 servings. The exact number can vary slightly depending on whether you use a tablespoon or a larger cookie scoop.
recipe_text:
Title: Classic No-Bake Cookies
Description: These classic no-bake cookies are rich, chocolatey, and incredibly easy to make in just one saucepan. With cocoa, peanut butter, and oats, they create a fudgy, chewy treat that sets perfectly without ever turning on the oven.
Prep: 5 min
Cook: 10 min
Total: 40 min
Servings/Yield: 24 servings
Calories: 210 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
- 1/2 cup peanut butter (creamy or chunky)
- 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
- 3 cups quick-cooking oats
- Pinch of salt

Instructions:
1. Line a baking sheet or clean countertop with wax paper or parchment paper.
2. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, combine butter, sugar, milk, and cocoa powder. Stir constantly until the mixture reaches a full rolling boil.
3. Once boiling, allow it to boil for exactly 1 minute, stirring continuously.
4. Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in peanut butter, vanilla bean paste, and salt until smooth.
5. Add the quick-cooking oats and mix until fully coated.
6. Using a tablespoon or cookie scoop, drop spoonfuls onto the prepared paper.
7. Let cookies cool and set at room temperature for 20–30 minutes, or refrigerate to firm up faster.

faq_text:
Q: Do No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites need to be baked?
A: No, these No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites are made without any baking. You mix the dough, roll it into balls, chill, and then coat in melted chocolate. The texture sets up in the refrigerator rather than the oven.

Q: How do I adjust the texture of No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites?
A: The milk controls how soft or firm the dough is. Use the full 1/3 cup for a softer, gooier dough, or use less milk for a firmer dough that’s easier to roll. Add the milk gradually so you can stop when the texture looks right.

Q: Why do I need to chill the cookie dough balls before coating them?
A: Chilling helps the dough balls firm up so they hold their shape when dipped or coated in chocolate. It also makes them easier to handle and reduces sticking. Place them on a parchment-lined tray in the fridge while you prep the chocolate.

Q: What’s the best way to get a smooth chocolate coating on No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites?
A: Melt the coating chocolate and, for best results, temper it for a glossy finish that snaps when set. Tempering can be done with a thermometer or a simplified tempering method. A smooth, fully melted chocolate also coats more evenly.

Q: Can I use milk chocolate or dark chocolate for the coating?
A: Yes, the recipe works with either milk chocolate or dark chocolate for the coating. Use 200 g total and melt it before dipping or coating the chilled bites. Choose whichever flavor you prefer for sweetness level.

Q: Can I substitute vanilla extract for vanilla bean paste in No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites?
A: Yes, you can use vanilla extract instead of vanilla bean paste. The vanilla is mixed in after creaming the butter and sugars, so either form will blend in easily. Keep the amount the same as listed for the paste.

Q: How many No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites does this recipe make?
A: This recipe is written for 24 servings. The number you get can vary slightly depending on how large you roll the dough balls. Using heaped teaspoons as directed helps keep the sizes consistent.

Q: Why is my cookie dough too dry or crumbly?
A: If the dough feels dry or crumbly, it likely needs a bit more milk to bring it together. Add the milk gradually, mixing between additions, until a soft dough forms. The final dough should be easy to scoop and roll into balls.
recipe_text:
Title: No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 10 min
Total: 15 min
Servings/Yield: 24 servings
Calories: 190 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Desserts

Ingredients:
- 2 sticks (227 g) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup (150 g) white sugar
- ¾ cup (165 g) light brown sugar
- 2 cups (250 g) all-purpose flour
- ⅓ cup milk
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
- ⅔ cup (120 g) mini chocolate chips
- 200 g milk or dark chocolate (for coating)

Instructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter, white sugar, and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the vanilla bean paste and mix until fully incorporated.
3. Stir in the flour and salt until a soft dough begins to form.
4. Gradually add the milk, mixing in between additions. Adjust the amount based on your desired dough texture—use all the milk for a soft and gooey dough, or less for a firmer texture.
5. Fold in the mini chocolate chips until evenly distributed throughout the dough.
6. Scoop out heaped teaspoons of dough and roll into small balls using your hands.
7. Place the dough balls on a parchment-lined tray and refrigerate while you prepare the chocolate coating.
8. For best results, temper the chocolate using a thermometer or simplified method to ensure a glossy finish that snaps when set.
9. If not tempering, melt the chocolate in 30-second intervals in the microwave, stirring between each until fully melted and smooth.
10. Dip each chilled cookie dough ball into the melted chocolate using a fork or dipping tool.
11. Allow excess chocolate to drip off before placing the bite onto a lined baking sheet.
12. Optional: Use a chocolate mold for a more uniform finish.
13. Let the chocolate set at room temperature or place in the refrigerator to speed up the process.

faq_text:
Q: How do I make a clean raspberry swirl in Raspberry Swirl Cheesecake?
A: Dollop the sweetened raspberry puree over the top of the cheesecake batter in several spots, then drag a toothpick or skewer through the dollops in gentle loops. Avoid over-swirling, which can blend the puree into the batter and muddy the pattern. Swirl only on the surface after the filling is in the pan.

Q: Why does this Raspberry Swirl Cheesecake recipe use sour cream?
A: Sour cream adds richness and a subtle tang that balances the sweetness of the cheesecake and raspberries. It also helps create a smoother, creamier texture in the baked filling. Mix it in after the cream cheese and sugar are beaten smooth.

Q: Do I need to bring the cream cheese to room temperature first?
A: Yes, room-temperature cream cheese blends much more smoothly and reduces the chance of lumps in the batter. Cold cream cheese can stay clumpy even after mixing, which affects the final texture. Let it soften before beating with the sugar.

Q: How can I tell when Raspberry Swirl Cheesecake is done baking?
A: Bake until the center is almost set, meaning it still has a slight jiggle but isn’t liquid. The recipe’s timing is 35–40 minutes at 325°F (163°C) in a 9x13-inch pan. The cheesecake will continue to firm up as it cools and chills.

Q: Why does the recipe call for chilling the cheesecake before serving?
A: Refrigerating for at least 3 hours allows the cheesecake to fully set and slice cleanly. It also improves the texture, making it denser and creamier. Chilling after cooling is an important step for this style of baked cheesecake.

Q: What does the flour do in the cheesecake filling?
A: A small amount of all-purpose flour helps the cheesecake hold together and gives the filling a slightly more stable structure. It can also reduce the chance of the center being overly soft. Fold it in after the eggs and raspberry puree are incorporated.

Q: Can I make Raspberry Swirl Cheesecake ahead of time?
A: Yes, this cheesecake is a good make-ahead dessert because it needs time to chill and set. Bake it, let it cool, then refrigerate it for at least 3 hours before serving. Add whipped cream, fresh raspberries, and powdered sugar just before serving for the best look.

Q: How should I store Raspberry Swirl Cheesecake?
A: Store the cheesecake covered in the refrigerator to keep it fresh and prevent it from absorbing odors. If you’ve added whipped cream and fresh raspberries, they should also be kept chilled. For best texture and neat slices, serve it cold.
recipe_text:
Title: Raspberry Swirl Cheesecake
Description: This Raspberry Swirl Cheesecake is rich, creamy, and easy to make—no agar-agar powder required. With a buttery graham cracker crust and vibrant raspberry puree swirls, it’s perfect for parties, holidays, or just a special treat.
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 40 min
Total: 4 hr
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 320 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 ½ cups graham cracker crumbs
- ⅓ cup granulated sugar
- 6 tbsp unsalted butter, melted
- 1 (8 oz) block cream cheese, room temperature
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 tsp vanilla bean paste
- 4 large eggs
- 2 tbsp all-purpose flour
- ¼ cup raspberry puree
- ½ cup raspberry puree (for swirl)
- 2 tbsp sugar (for swirl)
- Whipped cream
- Fresh raspberries
- Powdered sugar (for dusting)

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (163°C).
2. Mix graham cracker crumbs, ⅓ cup sugar, and melted butter. Press into a 9x13 inch pan. Chill while preparing filling.
3. In a bowl, beat cream cheese and 1 cup sugar until smooth. Mix in sour cream and vanilla bean paste. Add eggs one at a time, then fold in flour and ¼ cup raspberry puree.
4. Pour filling over crust. Dollop raspberry swirl mixture on top and swirl using a toothpick.
5. Bake for 35–40 minutes or until the center is almost set. Let cool, then refrigerate for at least 3 hours.
6. Before serving, top with whipped cream, fresh raspberries, and dust with powdered sugar.

recipe_text:
Title: Easy No-Bake Samoa Cookies
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 10 min
Total: 25 min
Servings/Yield: 6 servings
Calories: 180 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 package fudge stripe shortbread cookies (about 20-24 cookies)
- 1 (11 oz) package caramel bits
- 3 tablespoons heavy cream
- 2 cups toasted coconut*
- 2 squares chocolate CandiQuik

Instructions:
1. Line a baking sheet with foil and arrange the fudge stripe shortbread cookies, stripe-side up, about 1-2 inches apart. Set aside.
2. In a microwave-safe bowl, combine the caramel bits and heavy cream. Microwave on high for about 30 seconds, then stir. Microwave for an additional 15-20 seconds, stirring until smooth. Fold in the toasted coconut until well combined.
3. Quickly spoon a tablespoon of the coconut caramel mixture onto each cookie, spreading it to cover the surface. Repeat with the remaining cookies.
4. To toast coconut: Preheat your oven to 300°F. Spread the coconut evenly in a foil-lined baking pan. Bake for 5 minutes, then stir. Continue baking for another 5-7 minutes, stirring once more. Bake for an additional 3-5 minutes, watching closely until the coconut is toasted.

recipe_text:
Title: Healthy Cookie Dough Bark
Description: Healthy cookie dough bark is a quick and easy no-bake dessert for cookie dough lovers! The edible cookie dough is made with almond flour, maple syrup, coconut oil, and cashew butter. The easy recipe is vegan, gluten-free, and refined sugar-free. It's topped with a layer of rich melted chocolate. You can customize this raw dessert with ingredient swaps for oat flour, honey, and more!
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 20 min
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 280 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 and 1/2 cups almond flour
- 1/4 cup cashew butter (or peanut butter)
- 1/4 cup maple syrup
- 2 tbsp melted coconut oil
- 1 tsp vanilla bean paste
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1/4 cup chocolate chips
- 1 cup chocolate chips
- 1 tsp coconut oil
- Flaky sea salt, optional

Instructions:
1. In a bowl, add almond flour, cashew butter, maple syrup, melted coconut oil, vanilla, and salt. Mix together until evenly combined. Then, stir in the chocolate chips.
2. Place parchment paper on a cutting board or large plate. Transfer the cookie dough to the parchment paper. Using a silicone spatula or clean hands, firmly press the cookie dough to flatten (about 1/2-inch thick). Set aside.
3. In a small bowl, add chocolate chips and coconut oil. Microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring between, until the chocolate is fully melted.
4. Pour and spread the melted chocolate over the cookie dough. Sprinkle flaky sea salt on top.
5. Chill cookie dough bark in the freezer for 10 minutes to set. After, chop into large chunks. Enjoy!

recipe_text:
Title: Paleo, Keto, & Sugar Free No Bake Cookies
Description: These paleo, keto, and sugar free no-bake cookies are a delicious, healthy, fill-your-tummy-treat; perfect for a little indulgence without wrecking your diet.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 10 min
Servings/Yield: 18 servings
Calories: 269 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 cup almond butter ((or peanut butter, but not paleo))
- ½ cup Lakanto maple syrup flavored monkfruit
- ½ cup coconut oil
- 5 tablespoons raw cacao
- 1 tablespoon vanilla bean paste
- 2 1/2 cups shredded coconut ((or gluten free oats, but not paleo or low carb))

Instructions:
1. Heat the peanut butter, syrup, cacao, vanilla, and coconut oil together on the stove over med-low heat.
2. Stir it continually it so it melts quickly and blends well, but doesn't burn to the bottom or sides.
3. Remove the mixture from the heat, and add in the shredded coconut.
4. Gently stir in the shredded coconut, and mix well to ensure there are no clumps.
5. Let the cookie mixture cool until it thickens, approximately 20-30 minutes. Stirring helps it cool faster.
6. Using a large spoon, put scoops of the cookie mixture on a parchment-lined cookie sheet.
7. Put in the fridge until solid. Cookies may be refrigerated for an hour, or put in the freezer for 20 minutes.

recipe_text:
Title: Classic No-Bake Cookies
Description: These classic no-bake cookies are rich, chocolatey, and incredibly easy to make in just one saucepan. With cocoa, peanut butter, and oats, they create a fudgy, chewy treat that sets perfectly without ever turning on the oven.
Prep: 5 min
Cook: 10 min
Total: 27 min
Servings/Yield: 24 servings
Calories: 210 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
- 1/2 cup peanut butter (creamy or chunky)
- 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
- 3 cups quick-cooking oats
- Pinch of salt

Instructions:
1. Line a baking sheet or clean countertop with wax paper or parchment paper.
2. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, combine butter, sugar, milk, and cocoa powder. Stir constantly until the mixture reaches a full rolling boil.
3. Once boiling, allow it to boil for exactly 1 minute, stirring continuously.
4. Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in peanut butter, vanilla bean paste, and salt until smooth.
5. Add the quick-cooking oats and mix until fully coated.
6. Using a tablespoon or cookie scoop, drop spoonfuls onto the prepared paper.
7. Let cookies cool and set at room temperature for 20–30 minutes, or refrigerate to firm up faster.

recipe_text:
Title: No-Bake Cookie Dough Bites
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 15 min
Servings/Yield: 24 servings
Calories: 180 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Desserts

Ingredients:
- 2 sticks (227 g) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup (150 g) white sugar
- ¾ cup (165 g) light brown sugar
- 2 cups (250 g) all-purpose flour
- ⅓ cup milk
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
- ⅔ cup (120 g) mini chocolate chips
- 200 g milk or dark chocolate (for coating)

Instructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter, white sugar, and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the vanilla bean paste and mix until fully incorporated.
3. Stir in the flour and salt until a soft dough begins to form.
4. Gradually add the milk, mixing in between additions. Adjust the amount based on your desired dough texture—use all the milk for a soft and gooey dough, or less for a firmer texture.
5. Fold in the mini chocolate chips until evenly distributed throughout the dough.
6. Scoop out heaped teaspoons of dough and roll into small balls using your hands.
7. Place the dough balls on a parchment-lined tray and refrigerate while you prepare the chocolate coating.
8. For best results, temper the chocolate using a thermometer or simplified method to ensure a glossy finish that snaps when set.
9. If not tempering, melt the chocolate in 30-second intervals in the microwave, stirring between each until fully melted and smooth.
10. Dip each chilled cookie dough ball into the melted chocolate using a fork or dipping tool.
11. Allow excess chocolate to drip off before placing the bite onto a lined baking sheet.
12. Optional: Use a chocolate mold for a more uniform finish.
13. Let the chocolate set at room temperature or place in the refrigerator to speed up the process.

recipe_text:
Title: Oreo Mousse Cake
Description: This rich and creamy Oreo Mousse Cake is easy to make and full of chocolatey goodness making it the perfect dessert for any occasion.
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 255 min
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 514 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 3 oz Oreo Cookies (crushed (1 package - standard Oreos, not double stuffed))
- â  cup butter (melted - unsalted)
- 8 oz semi-sweet chocolate chips (melted)
- 3 cups heavy whipping cream
- Â½ cups confectionerâs sugar

Instructions:
1. Combine the crushed Oreos and melted butter in a mixing bowl. Mix them together until the crumbs are moist.
2. Press the mixture into the bottom of a lined 9-inch springform pan. Chill in the freezer until ready to fill.
3. Melt the chocolate chips in a medium microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments (stirring in between each round) until melted and stir until smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.
4. Whip the heavy cream and confectionerâs sugar in the bowl of a stand mixer with the whisk attachment on medium speed until stiff peaks. (this works best if the bowl is chilled prior to whipping)
5. Gently fold half of the whipped cream into the slightly cooled melted chocolate until combined.
6. Transfer the chocolate mixture into the spring form pan with the Oreo crust. Spread evenly to the edges.
7. Spread the remaining whipped cream evenly over the chocolate layer.
8. Sprinkle with additional crushed Oreo pieces.
9. Cover loosely and refrigerate for approximately 4 hours or until set.

recipe_text:
Title: Easy No-Bake Samoa Cookies
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 10 min
Total: 25 min
Servings/Yield: 6 servings
Calories: 180 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 package fudge stripe shortbread cookies (about 20-24 cookies)
- 1 (11 oz) package caramel bits
- 3 tablespoons heavy cream
- 2 cups toasted coconut*
- 2 squares chocolate CandiQuik

Instructions:
1. Line a baking sheet with foil and arrange the fudge stripe shortbread cookies, stripe-side up, about 1-2 inches apart. Set aside.
2. In a microwave-safe bowl, combine the caramel bits and heavy cream. Microwave on high for about 30 seconds, then stir. Microwave for an additional 15-20 seconds, stirring until smooth. Fold in the toasted coconut until well combined.
3. Quickly spoon a tablespoon of the coconut caramel mixture onto each cookie, spreading it to cover the surface. Repeat with the remaining cookies.
4. To toast coconut: Preheat your oven to 300°F. Spread the coconut evenly in a foil-lined baking pan. Bake for 5 minutes, then stir. Continue baking for another 5-7 minutes, stirring once more. Bake for an additional 3-5 minutes, watching closely until the coconut is toasted.

recipe_text:
Title: No-Bake Cheesecake
Description: No-Bake Cheesecake has never been so easy or delicious! A silky smooth cream cheese filling surrounded by a buttery graham cracker crust. Enjoy plain or with any desired toppings with no oven required.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 250 min
Servings/Yield: 8 servings
Calories: 516 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 1/4 cups graham cracker crumbs
- 6 tablespoons butter (melted)
- 16 oz cream cheese (softened (two 8 oz packages))
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 2 teaspoons vanilla bean paste
- 1 cup heavy cream (cold)
- 1 cup canned pie filling (cherry, blueberry, etc. (or topping of choice))

Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, stir graham cracker crumbs and melted butter together to moisten. Press into an 8 or 9-inch glass pie plate and refrigerate.
2. In a large bowl, mix cream cheese and granulated sugar together until smooth and well combined. Scrape the sides and mix again to ensure there are no lumps. Stir in vanilla bean paste.
3. With the mixer on, add cream in four increments, letting it whip into the cream cheese after each addition. Whip 1 minute until thick and light. Scrape the sides to ensure even mixing and whip again if needed.
4. Pour into prepared pie shell and smooth the top. Refrigerate 2 hours to overnight, covered in plastic wrap.
5. To serve, cut into pieces and top with two tablespoons of pie filling (or whipped cream) if desired.

recipe_text:
Title: Easy No Bake Chocolate Cheesecake
Description: Easy No Bake Chocolate Cheesecake is a decadent dessert that requires no baking—perfect for those who want to indulge in rich flavors without the fuss.
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 0 hr
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 320 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 2 3/4 cups Oreo cookie crumbs
- 5 tbsp melted butter
- 24 oz cream cheese, room temperature
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 3 tbsp cocoa powder
- 8 oz semi-sweet chocolate, melted
- 1 1/4 cups heavy whipping cream
- 3/4 cup powdered sugar

Instructions:
1. For the crust: Mix Oreo crumbs with melted butter and press into a springform pan. Chill while preparing the filling.
2. For the filling: Beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth. Mix in cocoa powder and melted chocolate. Whip heavy cream with powdered sugar until soft peaks form, then fold into chocolate mixture.
3. Assemble: Pour filling over crust in springform pan. Smooth the top and refrigerate for at least 5 hours until set.
4. Before serving, whip additional heavy cream for topping and spread it on the cheesecake. Add desired toppings.

recipe_text:
Title: No-Bake Cheesecake
Description: This is truly the most perfect and easy no-bake cheesecake. By following this no-bake cheesecake recipe, you'll be gifted with a smooth, light, and creamy dessert that sets up beautifully in the refrigerator. The most important steps are whipping the heavy cream into stiff peaks before folding into the filling, and refrigerating the cheesecake overnight.
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 8 hr
Servings/Yield: 10 servings
Calories: 180 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 2 cups (240g) graham cracker crumbs (about 16 full sheet graham crackers)
- 1/3 cup (67g) packed light or dark brown sugar
- 1/2 cup (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, melted
- 1 and 1/4 cups (300ml) heavy cream or heavy whipping cream
- 24 ounces (678g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature
- 1/2 cup (100g) granulated sugar
- 2 Tablespoons (15g) confectioners’ sugar
- 1/4 cup (60g) sour cream, at room temperature
- 2 teaspoons lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon pure vanilla bean paste

Instructions:
1. Using a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, whip the cold heavy cream into stiff peaks on medium-high speed, about 4–5 minutes. Set aside.
2. Using your mixer on low speed or a silicone spatula, fold the whipped cream into the cheesecake filling until combined. This takes several turns of your silicone spatula. Combine slowly as you don’t want to deflate all the air in the whipped cream.
3. Remove crust from the freezer and spread filling into crust. Use an offset spatula to smooth down the top.
4. Cover tightly with plastic wrap or aluminum foil and refrigerate for at least 6–8 hours and up to 2 days. For best results, 12 hours is best. I chill mine overnight. The longer refrigerated, the nicer the no-bake cheesecake will set up.
5. Use a knife to loosen the chilled cheesecake from the rim of the springform pan, then remove the rim. Using a clean sharp knife, cut into slices for serving. For neat slices, wipe the knife clean between each slice.
6. Serve cheesecake with desired toppings (see Note). Cover and store leftover cheesecake in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

recipe_text:
Title: Oreo Mousse Cake
Description: This rich and creamy Oreo Mousse Cake is easy to make and full of chocolatey goodness making it the perfect dessert for any occasion.
Prep: 15 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 255 min
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 514 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 3 oz Oreo Cookies (crushed (1 package - standard Oreos, not double stuffed))
- â  cup butter (melted - unsalted)
- 8 oz semi-sweet chocolate chips (melted)
- 3 cups heavy whipping cream
- Â½ cups confectionerâs sugar

Instructions:
1. Combine the crushed Oreos and melted butter in a mixing bowl. Mix them together until the crumbs are moist.
2. Press the mixture into the bottom of a lined 9-inch springform pan. Chill in the freezer until ready to fill.
3. Melt the chocolate chips in a medium microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments (stirring in between each round) until melted and stir until smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.
4. Whip the heavy cream and confectionerâs sugar in the bowl of a stand mixer with the whisk attachment on medium speed until stiff peaks. (this works best if the bowl is chilled prior to whipping)
5. Gently fold half of the whipped cream into the slightly cooled melted chocolate until combined.
6. Transfer the chocolate mixture into the spring form pan with the Oreo crust. Spread evenly to the edges.
7. Spread the remaining whipped cream evenly over the chocolate layer.
8. Sprinkle with additional crushed Oreo pieces.
9. Cover loosely and refrigerate for approximately 4 hours or until set.

recipe_text:
Title: Creamy No Bake Biscoff Cheesecake with Crunchy Cookie Crust
Description: A silky, rich no bake cheesecake featuring a crunchy Biscoff cookie crust and a luscious caramel-spiced filling, perfect for an easy and impressive dessert.
Prep: 30 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 4 hr
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 320 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 200g (7oz) Biscoff cookies
- 6 tbsp (85g) unsalted butter, melted
- 16 oz (450g) cream cheese, softened
- 1 cup (250g) Biscoff spread
- 1 cup (240ml) heavy cream, cold
- 3/4 cup (90g) powdered sugar
- 1 tsp vanilla bean paste

Instructions:
1. Crush 200g (7oz) Biscoff cookies into fine crumbs using a rolling pin inside a ziplock bag.
2. Mix cookie crumbs with 6 tbsp (85g) melted unsalted butter until mixture resembles wet sand.
3. Press the crust mixture firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan and refrigerate while preparing the filling.
4. Beat 16 oz (450g) softened cream cheese until smooth and creamy (about 2 minutes).
5. Add 1 cup (250g) Biscoff spread and 3/4 cup (90g) powdered sugar to the cream cheese and mix until silky smooth.
6. In a separate bowl, whip 1 cup (240ml) cold heavy cream and 1 tsp vanilla bean paste to soft peaks.
7. Gently fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture slowly to keep it light and fluffy.
8. Pour the filling over the chilled cookie crust and smooth the top with a spatula.
9. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight for best results.
10. Before serving, run a warm knife around the edges, unlock the springform pan, and slice. Optionally drizzle extra Biscoff spread or garnish with crushed cookies.

recipe_text:
Title: Chocolate Chip No Bake Cheesecake
Description: This Chocolate Chip No Bake Cheesecake is a creamy, indulgent dessert that’s super easy to make and perfect for any occasion!
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 240 min
Servings/Yield: 8 servings
Calories: 180 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips (, coarsely chopped)
- 1 cup heavy whipping cream
- 20 ounces cream cheese (, softened)
- ¾ cup powdered sugar
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 tsp vanilla bean paste
- 1 cup mini chocolate chips
- 2 tbsp heavy whipping cream ((for topping))

Instructions:
1. Prepare the OREO crust as per the linked recipe and set aside to cool.
2. In a mixing bowl, whip 1 cup of heavy whipping cream on medium-high speed until stiff peaks form, about 3-4 minutes. Set aside.
3. In another bowl, combine softened cream cheese and powdered sugar. Beat until smooth and creamy.
4. Add sour cream and vanilla bean paste to the cream cheese mixture, mixing until fluffy.
5. Gently fold in the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture with a spatula.
6. Stir in the coarsely chopped semi-sweet chocolate chips and mini chocolate chips.
7. Pour the cheesecake filling into the prepared OREO crust, smoothing the top.
8. Refrigerate the cheesecake for at least 4 hours, or overnight for best results.
9. When ready to serve, whip 2 tablespoons of heavy cream until soft peaks form for topping.

recipe_text:
Title: No-Bake Cheesecake
Description: No-Bake Cheesecake has never been so easy or delicious! A silky smooth cream cheese filling surrounded by a buttery graham cracker crust. Enjoy plain or with any desired toppings with no oven required.
Prep: 10 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 250 min
Servings/Yield: 8 servings
Calories: 516 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 1/4 cups graham cracker crumbs
- 6 tablespoons butter (melted)
- 16 oz cream cheese (softened (two 8 oz packages))
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 2 teaspoons vanilla bean paste
- 1 cup heavy cream (cold)
- 1 cup canned pie filling (cherry, blueberry, etc. (or topping of choice))

Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, stir graham cracker crumbs and melted butter together to moisten. Press into an 8 or 9-inch glass pie plate and refrigerate.
2. In a large bowl, mix cream cheese and granulated sugar together until smooth and well combined. Scrape the sides and mix again to ensure there are no lumps. Stir in vanilla bean paste.
3. With the mixer on, add cream in four increments, letting it whip into the cream cheese after each addition. Whip 1 minute until thick and light. Scrape the sides to ensure even mixing and whip again if needed.
4. Pour into prepared pie shell and smooth the top. Refrigerate 2 hours to overnight, covered in plastic wrap.
5. To serve, cut into pieces and top with two tablespoons of pie filling (or whipped cream) if desired.

recipe_text:
Title: Easy No Bake Chocolate Cheesecake
Description: Easy No Bake Chocolate Cheesecake is a decadent dessert that requires no baking—perfect for those who want to indulge in rich flavors without the fuss.
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 0 hr
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 320 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 2 3/4 cups Oreo cookie crumbs
- 5 tbsp melted butter
- 24 oz cream cheese, room temperature
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 3 tbsp cocoa powder
- 8 oz semi-sweet chocolate, melted
- 1 1/4 cups heavy whipping cream
- 3/4 cup powdered sugar

Instructions:
1. For the crust: Mix Oreo crumbs with melted butter and press into a springform pan. Chill while preparing the filling.
2. For the filling: Beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth. Mix in cocoa powder and melted chocolate. Whip heavy cream with powdered sugar until soft peaks form, then fold into chocolate mixture.
3. Assemble: Pour filling over crust in springform pan. Smooth the top and refrigerate for at least 5 hours until set.
4. Before serving, whip additional heavy cream for topping and spread it on the cheesecake. Add desired toppings.

recipe_text:
Title: No-Bake Cheesecake
Description: This is truly the most perfect and easy no-bake cheesecake. By following this no-bake cheesecake recipe, you'll be gifted with a smooth, light, and creamy dessert that sets up beautifully in the refrigerator. The most important steps are whipping the heavy cream into stiff peaks before folding into the filling, and refrigerating the cheesecake overnight.
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 8 hr
Servings/Yield: 10 servings
Calories: 180 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 2 cups (240g) graham cracker crumbs (about 16 full sheet graham crackers)
- 1/3 cup (67g) packed light or dark brown sugar
- 1/2 cup (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, melted
- 1 and 1/4 cups (300ml) heavy cream or heavy whipping cream
- 24 ounces (678g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature
- 1/2 cup (100g) granulated sugar
- 2 Tablespoons (15g) confectioners’ sugar
- 1/4 cup (60g) sour cream, at room temperature
- 2 teaspoons lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon pure vanilla bean paste

Instructions:
1. Using a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, whip the cold heavy cream into stiff peaks on medium-high speed, about 4–5 minutes. Set aside.
2. Using your mixer on low speed or a silicone spatula, fold the whipped cream into the cheesecake filling until combined. This takes several turns of your silicone spatula. Combine slowly as you don’t want to deflate all the air in the whipped cream.
3. Remove crust from the freezer and spread filling into crust. Use an offset spatula to smooth down the top.
4. Cover tightly with plastic wrap or aluminum foil and refrigerate for at least 6–8 hours and up to 2 days. For best results, 12 hours is best. I chill mine overnight. The longer refrigerated, the nicer the no-bake cheesecake will set up.
5. Use a knife to loosen the chilled cheesecake from the rim of the springform pan, then remove the rim. Using a clean sharp knife, cut into slices for serving. For neat slices, wipe the knife clean between each slice.
6. Serve cheesecake with desired toppings (see Note). Cover and store leftover cheesecake in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

recipe_text:
Title: Creamy No Bake Biscoff Cheesecake with Crunchy Cookie Crust
Description: A silky, rich no bake cheesecake featuring a crunchy Biscoff cookie crust and a luscious caramel-spiced filling, perfect for an easy and impressive dessert.
Prep: 30 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 4 hr
Servings/Yield: 12 servings
Calories: 320 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 200g (7oz) Biscoff cookies
- 6 tbsp (85g) unsalted butter, melted
- 16 oz (450g) cream cheese, softened
- 1 cup (250g) Biscoff spread
- 1 cup (240ml) heavy cream, cold
- 3/4 cup (90g) powdered sugar
- 1 tsp vanilla bean paste

Instructions:
1. Crush 200g (7oz) Biscoff cookies into fine crumbs using a rolling pin inside a ziplock bag.
2. Mix cookie crumbs with 6 tbsp (85g) melted unsalted butter until mixture resembles wet sand.
3. Press the crust mixture firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan and refrigerate while preparing the filling.
4. Beat 16 oz (450g) softened cream cheese until smooth and creamy (about 2 minutes).
5. Add 1 cup (250g) Biscoff spread and 3/4 cup (90g) powdered sugar to the cream cheese and mix until silky smooth.
6. In a separate bowl, whip 1 cup (240ml) cold heavy cream and 1 tsp vanilla bean paste to soft peaks.
7. Gently fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture slowly to keep it light and fluffy.
8. Pour the filling over the chilled cookie crust and smooth the top with a spatula.
9. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight for best results.
10. Before serving, run a warm knife around the edges, unlock the springform pan, and slice. Optionally drizzle extra Biscoff spread or garnish with crushed cookies.

recipe_text:
Title: Chocolate Chip No Bake Cheesecake
Description: This Chocolate Chip No Bake Cheesecake is a creamy, indulgent dessert that’s super easy to make and perfect for any occasion!
Prep: 20 min
Cook: 12 min
Total: 240 min
Servings/Yield: 8 servings
Calories: 180 calories
Cuisine: American
Course: Dessert

Ingredients:
- 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips (, coarsely chopped)
- 1 cup heavy whipping cream
- 20 ounces cream cheese (, softened)
- ¾ cup powdered sugar
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 tsp vanilla bean paste
- 1 cup mini chocolate chips
- 2 tbsp heavy whipping cream ((for topping))

Instructions:
1. Prepare the OREO crust as per the linked recipe and set aside to cool.
2. In a mixing bowl, whip 1 cup of heavy whipping cream on medium-high speed until stiff peaks form, about 3-4 minutes. Set aside.
3. In another bowl, combine softened cream cheese and powdered sugar. Beat until smooth and creamy.
4. Add sour cream and vanilla bean paste to the cream cheese mixture, mixing until fluffy.
5. Gently fold in the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture with a spatula.
6. Stir in the coarsely chopped semi-sweet chocolate chips and mini chocolate chips.
7. Pour the cheesecake filling into the prepared OREO crust, smoothing the top.
8. Refrigerate the cheesecake for at least 4 hours, or overnight for best results.
9. When ready to serve, whip 2 tablespoons of heavy cream until soft peaks form for topping.
